title: LEGO Set Match Builder
tagline: Start with one brick and discover which owned sets it likely belongs to.
author: bcumbie
narrative: Build a LEGO helper where someone enters a single LEGO piece and the app cross-references it against the sets they own, tracks each set's condition such as fully built or missing pieces, and suggests which sets the piece most likely goes with.
description: Build a LEGO helper where someone enters a single LEGO piece and the app cross-references it against the sets they own, tracks each set's condition such as fully built or missing pieces, and suggests which sets the piece most likely goes with.

userStory.iWant: to enter one piece and compare it to the sets I already own
userStory.soThat: I can quickly figure out where the piece belongs and what sets may be incomplete

notes.title: First demo scope
notes.text: Start with a single piece lookup, owned set matching, and a simple status flag for built versus missing pieces.
notes.author: bcumbie

title: Record Album Inventory App
tagline: Track your vinyl collection with condition, value, and listening notes.
author: bcumbie
narrative: Build a personal vinyl catalog app where a collector can track album details, pressing variants, condition grades for sleeve and media, purchase history, and quick notes from each play session.
description: Build a personal vinyl catalog app where a collector can track album details, pressing variants, condition grades for sleeve and media, purchase history, and quick notes from each play session.

userStory.iWant: to organize my albums with condition and pressing details
userStory.soThat: I can manage my collection and spot duplicates before I buy

notes.title: MVP
notes.text: Start with artist, album title, year, genre, sleeve/media condition, and purchase price.
notes.author: bcumbie

title: Where Dat?
tagline: Store where things are now so you can find them later.
author: bcumbie
narrative: Build an app for everyday item memory: warranty cards in drawers, spare screws from furniture builds, holiday flags, and random stored stuff you forget. Save what the item is, exactly where you put it, and optional photo/context so later you can search and instantly find it.
description: Build an app for everyday item memory: warranty cards in drawers, spare screws from furniture builds, holiday flags, and random stored stuff you forget. Save what the item is, exactly where you put it, and optional photo/context so later you can search and instantly find it.

userStory.iWant: to log where I put important household items
userStory.soThat: I can quickly find them later instead of re-buying things I already have

notes.title: MVP fields
notes.text: Item name, exact location path, optional photo, and last-seen date should be the first release.
notes.author: bcumbie

title: Chill Appz
tagline: Escalating reminders so freezer drinks never explode again.
author: bcumbie
narrative: Build a silly but practical escalating notification app for quick-freeze drinks: start with timer/alarm, then escalate to text, email, call, and optionally call a friend if you ignore alerts. Track context like can vs six-pack, freezer fullness, starting temperature, and freezer type, then learn from results (too warm, perfect, too frozen) to improve future timing.
description: Build a silly but practical escalating notification app for quick-freeze drinks: start with timer/alarm, then escalate to text, email, call, and optionally call a friend if you ignore alerts. Track context like can vs six-pack, freezer fullness, starting temperature, and freezer type, then learn from results (too warm, perfect, too frozen) to improve future timing.

userStory.iWant: an escalating reminder system for drinks in the freezer
userStory.soThat: I can get them perfectly chilled without forgetting and making a mess

notes.title: Escalation ladder
notes.text: Timer alarm first, then SMS, then email, then call if no acknowledgement is received.
notes.author: bcumbie
